observations.evidence_basis: 6 primary cross-model audit verdicts (5 ACCEPTED, 1 ACCEPTED_WITH_NOTES for FP-012 Qwen); 1 post-fix audit confirming resolution (FP-012 DeepSeek); 7 total audit artifacts; 0 REJECTED verdicts across all audits
observations.summary: Both models produced accepted implementations for all three reviewed packets.

observations.evidence_basis: metrics.json fix_attempts fields and executor result correction sections for all 3 packets record corrections by both models
observations.summary: Both models required corrections in all three packets. Neither achieved first-pass success in any packet.

observations.evidence_basis: metrics-deepseek.json: 3 corrections (NOT_EVALUATED default, empty trust_tier default, truthy-check bug)
observations.summary: DeepSeek required 3 corrections in FP-010: two enum default errors and one truthy-check validation bug.

observations.evidence_basis: metrics-qwen.json: 1 correction (truthy-check bug); executor-result-qwen.md lines 65-68
observations.summary: Qwen required 1 correction in FP-010: same truthy-check validation bug as DeepSeek.

observations.evidence_basis: comparison-result.md: DeepSeek used text packet_id, Qwen used integer FK referencing packets(id)
observations.summary: Schema design diverged: DeepSeek used human-readable text packet_id, Qwen used integer foreign key. Comparison selected Qwen on relational integration fitness.

observations.evidence_basis: metrics.json: fix_attempts=1; admission evaluation returned outcomes in validation_state, caught pre-commit
observations.summary: DeepSeek required 1 correction in FP-011: admission/validation type conflation, caught before commit.

observations.evidence_basis: metrics.json: fix_attempts=2; comparison-result.md notes dead code cleanup and event naming documentation; observed cost $2.61
observations.summary: Qwen required 2 corrections in FP-011: dead code cleanup and event naming documentation. Observed execution cost $2.61 USD.

observations.evidence_basis: comparison-result.md: DeepSeek used validation_state/admission_state matching packet vocabulary
observations.summary: DeepSeek used packet vocabulary directly in event table columns.

observations.evidence_basis: executor-result-qwen.md lines 38-43: used validation_outcome/admission_outcome naming
observations.summary: Qwen used alternative validation_outcome/admission_outcome naming distinct from packet vocabulary.

observations.evidence_basis: executor-result-deepseek.md: updated src/db/schema.sql with canonical FP-011 definitions
observations.summary: DeepSeek updated the canonical schema file alongside the migration.

observations.evidence_basis: comparison-result.md: Qwen did not update src/db/schema.sql
observations.summary: Qwen did not update the canonical schema file.

observations.evidence_basis: metrics.json: observed_cost_usd=2.61
observations.summary: Qwen FP-011 execution incurred $2.61 USD in observed cost. No comparable cost data exists for DeepSeek FP-011.

observations.evidence_basis: postfix-audit-qwen-by-deepseek.md: 1 post-audit fix for defect carry-forward in comparison correction; initial verdict ACCEPTED_WITH_NOTES, post-fix ACCEPTED
observations.summary: DeepSeek required 1 post-audit correction in FP-012 for comparison correction defect carry-forward.

observations.evidence_basis: audit-deepseek-by-qwen.md: extensive correction adding explicit FK columns, model_identity, correction tables in migration 009; verdict ACCEPTED_WITH_NOTES
observations.summary: Qwen required extensive corrections in FP-012. Initial implementation used looser design; added 8 FK columns, model_identity, and 2 correction tables in migration 009.

observations.evidence_basis: audit-deepseek-by-qwen.md Note 1: getLatestTaskClassification and getLatestModelComparison return latest event row without merging corrections
observations.summary: Qwen's getLatest* functions do not merge correction data. Consumers needing effective corrected state must manually compose corrections. Design gap identified by audit.

observations.evidence_basis: audit-qwen-by-deepseek.md: self-referencing correction rows carry forward compared identifiers; initial defect carry-forward bug, fixed to carry forward prevEvent values
observations.summary: DeepSeek used self-referencing correction rows in event tables. Correction carry-forward now preserves all compared identifiers and defect fields.

observations.evidence_basis: FP-011: 57 tests (vs DeepSeek 40); FP-012: 107 tests (vs DeepSeek ~70)
observations.summary: Qwen consistently wrote more tests than DeepSeek in FP-011 and FP-012. In FP-010, DeepSeek wrote more (28 vs 24).

observations.evidence_basis: metrics-deepseek.json files_modified includes schema.sql in FP-011 and FP-012; Qwen modified schema.sql only in FP-012
observations.summary: DeepSeek showed a pattern of updating canonical schema across multiple packets. Limited inference from 3 packets.

observations.evidence_basis: FP-011 direct vocabulary use; FP-012 self-referencing correction model matches packet append-only language; Qwen naming divergence in FP-011
observations.summary: DeepSeek showed a pattern of matching packet vocabulary more directly. Limited inference from 2 packets where vocabulary was material.

observations.evidence_basis: All 6 primary cross-model audits and the 1 post-fix audit (7 total audit artifacts) confirmed no routing, ranking, cost optimization, leaderboards, dashboards, reports, or automatic model selection added
observations.summary: Both models preserved out-of-scope boundaries in all three packets with no violations found by any audit.

observations.evidence_basis: FP-010 and FP-011 audits confirm trust_tier, validation_state, admission_state remain independent axes; tests verify no collapsed status field
observations.summary: Both models preserved the three-axis state model (trust, validation, admission) without collapsing them into a single status field.

limitations: Only 3 packets reviewed. No inference generalizes beyond FP-010, FP-011, FP-012.
All 3 packets were PERSISTENCE-type tasks. No CLI, BUGFIX, REFACTOR, or ARCHITECTURE tasks were observed.
Cost data recorded only for Qwen FP-011 ($2.61). No cost comparison possible.
Each model audited the other. No independent auditor. Audit quality may interact with executor performance.
No evidence records were independently reproduced.
All evidence carries trust_tier TIER_0_UNTRUSTED or TIER_1_SELF_REPORTED. No evidence has been admitted through FP-011 pathways.
Correction classification subjectivity. Some corrections may be equivalently design refinements under different criteria.
Selection basis varied by packet (schema_integration_quality, packet_vocabulary_and_schema_alignment, COMPOSITE). No consistent selection basis across packets.
non_decisions: No global model ranking is created.
No routing policy or automatic model selection is established.
No cost optimization rule is derived from the single cost data point.
No leaderboard or aggregate scoring is produced.
No dashboard or report logic is added.
No workflow orchestration changes are implied.
